Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Service Of Deposition Subpoenas And For Production Of Documents And Things
Off Calendar Feb-25-2016 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Stephen Tigerman and Lisa Brokaw of HAROWITZ & TIGERMAN, LLP (415-788-1588) appearing for plaintiffs / moving parties Avis Pratt, individually and successor-in-interest to Irving Pratt, decedent, et al.
Angela V. Sayre of LITCHFIELD CAVO LLP (626-683-5080), appearing for non-parties / opposing parties Litchfield Cavo LLP and G. David Rubin, Esq.

RULING:
NON-PARTIES G. RUBIN, ESQ. AND LITCHFIELD CAVO LLP'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF DEPOSITION SUBPOENAS AND FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS AND THINGS

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted as amended: Non-parties Litchfield Cavo LLP and G. David Rubin, Esq.'s motion to quash service of deposition subpoenas and for production of documents and things is denied. Non-parties Litchfield Cavo LLP and G. David Rubin, Esq. are ordered to prepare a privilege log in 10 days (jointly or otherwise determined by non-parties) as set forth in Catalina Island Yacht Club v. Superior Court (2015) 242 Cal.App.4th 1116, 1129. The log will be due on March 7, 2016. Further, if a party argues that something is unduly burdensome, that it is not a sufficient objection. The proper ground for the objection is "oppression." It is not enough that the demand will be burdensome to answer. It must be shown that the burden of answering is so unjust that it amounts to oppression. In determining whether the burden is unjust a weighing process is required. It must appear that the amount of work required to answer the questions is so great, and the utility of the information sought so minimal, that it would defeat the ends of justice to require the answers. West Pico Furniture Co. of Los Angeles v. Sup. Ct. (Pacific Finance Loans) (1961) 56 Cal. 2d 407. Order signed in open court. Judge: Garrett L. Wong; Reporter: Noel Carter Degnan of US LEGAL SUPPORT, CSR #6921 (415-487-9008) (503/GLW)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Depositions Of Defendant American Lafrance, Llcs Person(S) Most Knowledgeable And Custodian(S) Of Records And Production Of Documents And Things
Off Calendar Mar-13-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Steven M. Harowitz of HAROWITZ & TIGERMAN, LLP (415-788-1588) appearing for plaintiffs / moving parties - Avis Pratt / Teresa Robison, et al.
Talar Tavlian of LITCHFIELD CAVO LLP (626-683-1100), appearing via CourtCall for defendant / opposing party American LaFrance, LLC

RULING:
PLAINTIFFS' MOTION TO COMPEL DEPOSITIONS OF DEFENDANT AMERICAN LAFRANCE, LLC'S PERSON(S) MOST KNOWLEDGEABLE AND CUSTODIAN(S) OF RECORDS AND PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS AND THINGS

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted as modified: Plaintiffs' motion to compel depositions of defendant American LaFrance, LLC's person(s) most knowledgeable / custodian(s) of records and production of documents and things is granted. Plaintiffs have a right to depose defendant. (C.C.P. 2025.010.) Plaintiffs properly met and conferred prior to filing their motion. Defendant's filing of objections to the deposition notice does not bar plaintiffs from bringing a motion to compel. Defendant did not demonstrate that its status is such that this court cannot compel it to produce a deponent. The deposition must conclude no later than April 21, 2014. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Kyle McLean of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #13787 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/TLJ)
